apply: Calculate the internal energy change (ΔU) for a system undergoing a thermodynamic process
Determine the entropy change (ΔS) for a system undergoing a thermodynamic process
Apply the first law of thermodynamics to solve problems related to heat, work, and internal energy
analyze: Analyze the efficiency of a heat engine and its relation to the environment
Compare the thermodynamic properties of different systems and processes
Critically evaluate the role of thermodynamics in various industrial and environmental applications
remember: Identify the laws of thermodynamics and their statements
List the properties of a system and their representation in thermodynamic equations
Recall the types of thermodynamic processes and their characteristics
understand: Explain the significance of temperature and its relation to heat and work
Describe the concept of internal energy and its relation to the state of a system
Understand the concept of entropy and its relation to disorder and randomness

ncert_objectives: Explain the laws of thermodynamics and their applications.
Define the terms - system, surroundings, and universe and explain the types of systems.
Explain the concept of internal energy, enthalpy and entropy and their measurement.
board_exam_objectives: The first law of thermodynamics: Internal energy, heat and work.
The second law of thermodynamics: Spontaneity, entropy and Gibbs energy.
Thermodynamic processes: Isothermal, adiabatic, isochoric and isobaric processes.
